decisionGate_frontier.why: Your v5 abstention read used the SINGLE-alpha report (alpha=0.02 -> answers 100%, stays ~0.93, target 0.98 -> looks un-shippable). That is the known single-point artifact: one alpha can answer ~everything and never reach the bar while a STRICTER point on the frontier clears it at lower coverage. The decisive read is the FRONTIER sweep, which your run did not emit.
decisionGate_frontier.actionTaken: I deployed the frontier+peft-monkeypatch certify_lowram.py to spark-bridge (commit 49955433, blob 3a54e94a) and PRE-STAGED the re-cert as bridge/commands/2026-07-01-claude-web-cert-v5-frontier.json (commit c9772cb0). The idle poller will run it autonomously on the next idle tick: cert-only --bench-b --execute, adapter /home/tomyimkc/sophia-bridge/training/lora/checkpoints/olmoe-qat-spark-v5, CERT_NEVAL=256, CERT_OUT=agi-proof/benchmark-results/nvfp4-v5-frontier.json.
decisionGate_frontier.read: It emits out[abstention_frontier]: quant top1-top2 margin thresholds swept on the CALIB split, coverage vs answered_top1 MEASURED out-of-sample on the disjoint TEST split, target_answered=0.97 (the cert bar, slightly more lenient than your alpha=0.02 -> 0.98). shippable_operating_point != None => v5+abstention is honestly shippable NOW (serve the confident tokens, abstain on the near-ties) and v6 becomes optional. None => the margin does not separate the argmax-flips -> abstention cannot rescue v5 -> v6 QAT is the confirmed path. THIS is the v6 GO/NO-GO gate.
decisionGate_frontier.ifYouGetThereFirst: If your poller runs it before you read this, just surface out[abstention_frontier][shippable_operating_point] (coverage + answered_top1) into from-mac/STATUS.json + a ledger row. Do NOT flip any recipe candidate->adopted from it; canClaimAGI stays false.
